item.title: Florida pastor sues OpenAI over dangerous medical advice he got from ChatGPT
item.description: A pastor in Florida is suing OpenAI after he used ChatGPT for medical advice and received suggestions that he claims caused him to delay necessary treatment. Jo Ling Kent reports.

item.title: What to know as U.S. completes 12th straight night of Iran strikes
item.description: The U.S. launched an 12th night of strikes against Iran Wednesday, hours after President Trump attended the dignified transfer of four service members killed in the war. CBS News' Eleanor Watson reports.

item.title: U.S. and Saudi Arabia sign nuclear cooperation deal
item.description: The U.S. and Saudi Arabia signed a deal for U.S. companies to run nuclear power on Saudi soil, the U.S. Department of Energy announced Wednesday.

item.title: U.S. launches more Iran strikes as threats to oil trade deepen
item.description: President Trump threatened to bomb an Iranian bridge or power plant for every ship attacked in the Strait of Hormuz, as one shipping insider says "nothing is going through."

item.title: Here's what's in the U.S.-Saudi Arabia nuclear deal
item.description: The U.S. and Saudi Arabia signed a new nuclear agreement Wedesday that could help the Middle Eastern country diverisify its energy sources. Margaret Brennan has more.

item.title: Trump attends dignified transfer of soldiers killed in Iranian attacks
item.description: President Trump attended a dignified transfer honoring the four U.S. Army soldiers killed in the Middle East in recent days during the ongoing war with Iran.

item.title: Trump attends dignified transfer for 4 dead U.S. soldiers, 12th night of strikes launched
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday oversaw the dignified transfer of the remains of four U.S. soldiers killed in the war on Iran. The U.S. launched another round of strikes on Iran Wednesday night. Weijia Jiang reports.

item.title: False rocket launcher report terrifies pilot landing at New York City's LaGuardia Airport
item.description: A Delta Airlines pilot coming in to land a flight with more than 100 people on board faced terrifying moments over LaGuardia Airport on Tuesday night after an anonymous caller falsely reported a rocket launcher on the ground. Tom Hanson has the story.

item.title: CBS News crew captured during Tiananmen Square massacre (1989)
item.description: CBS News correspondent Richard Roth and photographer Derek Williams were detained by Chinese authorities during the Tiananmen Square massacre in June 1989. They would eventually be released, relatively unharmed. These were the reports they filed through the chaos.

item.title: Florida police spray bullets at murder suspect's vehicle following car chase
item.description: Florida police fired a barrage of bullets at a vehicle belonging to a man wanted for the murder of a mother of four after he led them on a wild car chase. The suspect was taken into custody. Cristian Benavides has more.

item.title: House passes bill limiting lawmakers' stock trades
item.description: The House on Wednesday passed a bill to restrict lawmakers from stock trading, along with the National Defense Authorization Act. Julia Manchester, White House correspondent for The Hill, and Dave Levinthal, senior editor for NOTUS, join "The Takeout" to discuss.

item.title: ICE detention bookings climbed to highest level of Trump's 2nd term in June
item.description: June marked the highest number of people booked into immigration detention since President Trump returned to office. CBS News immigration correspondent Camilo Montoya-Galvez has the details.

item.title: Mayor who survived assassination attempt shot dead in town hall
item.description: Mayor Valentin Lavin, who was allied to President Claudia Sheinbaum's leftist party, had already survived an armed attack in January, according to local media reports.

item.title: Salazar responds to Sheinbaum's claim that he lied about "El Mayo" capture: "Truth is the truth"
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum has accused Ken Salazar, who served as both a senator and the U.S. ambassador to Mexico, of lying about the 2024 capture of Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada. Salazar, who writes about the issue in his new book, "Borderlands," joins "The Takeout" with his response.

item.title: Machine learning professor breaks down OpenAI model's hack of another AI company
item.description: OpenAI, the developer behind ChatGPT, says one of its artificial intelligence models went rogue and hacked another AI company. Neil Lawrence, professor of machine learning at the University of Cambridge, joins to assess the claim.

item.title: Raffensperger reacts to improper noncitizen voter registrations in New Jersey, DHS claims
item.description: New Jersey Gov. Mikie Sherrill said on Tuesday that, three years ago, a computer glitch resulted in some 6,600 noncitizens being registered to vote in the state. Georgia Secretary of State Brad Raffensperger joins to discuss.

item.title: Trump administration officially announces nuclear cooperation agreement with Saudi Arabia
item.description: The Trump administration announced a landmark agreement with Saudi Arabia on Wednesday that allows the Middle Eastern country to develop a civilian nuclear program on its soil. CBS News senior White House correspondent Weijia Jiang has the details.

item.title: Secret Service warns of high threat levels before White House Correspondents' Dinner
item.description: Secret Service officials warned nationwide threats have reached an unprecedented level since January, nearly 40% higher than during the same period last year.

item.title: Council handpicked by Trump to gut review process of historic-preservation projects
item.description: The Advisory Council for Historic Preservation, whose members were mostly handpicked by President Trump, is poised to end mandatory state review and public comment on projects affecting historical properties.

item.title: Sneak a peek at this holiday season's hottest new toys
item.description: While the holiday season is still months away, it's already prime time for the toy industry. Bradley Blackburn got a look at some of the new items that could be on kids' lists in December.

item.title: New technology could restore movement, sensation for millions of paralyzed patients
item.description: New findings identified a technology that could restore movement and sensation for millions of paralyzed people living with spinal cord injuries. Bradley Blackburn has the story.

item.title: Trump and Iran trade threats to attack energy infrastructure as U.S. completes 11th night of strikes
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday said that for every Iranian drone or missile attack in the Strait of Hormuz, the U.S. will "bomb and destroy one bridge or power plant." Iran said if the U.S. hits civilian targets it will seek to halt all oil flow from the region. CBS News' Charlie D'Agata and Samantha Vinograd have the latest.

item.title: First beluga whales from Marineland arrive in Texas as rescue underway
item.description: Two beluga whales settled into their new home at SeaWorld San Antonio this week following an international relocation mission from shuttered Canadian theme park Marineland.

item.title: Latest on U.S.-Saudi nuclear deal, Trump threats against Iranian infrastructure
item.description: CBS News National security analyst Aaron MacLean breaks down President Trump's threats against Iranian infrastructure and the United States' new nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia.

item.title: House and Senate take different paths to avoid shutdown before midterms
item.description: Another deadline to fund the government is looming this fall in Congress, and lawmakers seem eager to avoid another costly impasse ahead of the midterms.

item.title: DOJ sues over immersion water heaters that can burst into flames
item.description: The Justice Department is trying to force the two Chinese companies to recall immersion water heaters used for bathtubs and pools.

item.title: Newsom's office called our diaper contract reporting "demonstrably false." State records show what happened
item.description: Gov. Newsom's office says the no-bid deal was "competitive," the records weren't delayed, and our reporting is false. The state's own database, deadlines, and the Legislature's budget bills say otherwise. Here's the point-by-point review.

item.title: 3,000-year-old human remains found underneath volcanic ash
item.description: The ancient remains were found face down and will undergo radiocarbon dating to determine their age. DNA analysis will also be carried out to determine the person's sex.

item.title: OpenAI says its technology, on its own, hacked another AI company
item.description: ChatGPT maker OpenAI says its artificial intelligence system hacked into another AI company on its own in what the company called an "unprecedented cyber incident."

item.title: U.S. soldier killed in Iraq identified as 30-year-old from North Carolina
item.description: Sgt. Michael Emmanuel Swinton, 30, of North Carolina, was "decommissioning ammunition and an accident happened," Secretary of State Marco Rubio said.
